name: The Cove at Atlantis Paradise Island

content.verdict: The Cove is the grown-up corner of Atlantis — an all-suite oceanfront tower with its own adults-preferred Cain pool, day cabanas, and a separate vibe from the family Royal Towers. It's the smartest way to get the full Atlantis package (Aquaventure, marine habitats, dolphin encounters, casino, two dozen restaurants) without the family-vacation chaos in your sightline.
content.best_room: Book a Cove Ocean Suite on a high floor — 700 sq ft of ocean-facing space, separate living area, and panoramic balconies overlooking Cove Beach. If budget allows, the Sapphire or Royal Reserve Suites unlock private check-in, butler-style concierge, and elevated club lounge access.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and Cain pool sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Land in Nassau, transfer to Paradise Island, settle into your Cove Ocean Suite. Spend the afternoon at the Cain at the Cove adults-only pool with frozen cocktails, then dinner at Nobu inside the casino building.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Aquaventure waterpark day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Spend the day on Aquaventure's 141-acre water playground — the Leap of Faith slide, the Power Tower, the Current river. Lunch at Carmine's casual outpost, late afternoon back at Cain pool, dinner at Fish by Jose Andres.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cove Beach and spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow morning on Cove Beach with butler service, private cabana with chilled towels and fruit. Couples massage at Mandara Spa in the afternoon. Sunset cocktails at Sip Sip lounge, then dinner at Cafe Martinique by Jean-Georges.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Marine habitat and dolphin encounter
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning swim with dolphins at Dolphin Cay, then snorkel the Ruins Lagoon among rays and sharks. Afternoon at the casino or shopping at Marina Village. Dinner at Olives by Todd English.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Day trip to Blue Lagoon Island
itinerary_7_nights.description: Quick boat transfer to Blue Lagoon Island for swimming with stingrays or simply hammock time on a quieter beach. Return for a late lunch at Mosaic, then live music at the Atlantis Theatre.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Nassau and Junkanoo Beach
itinerary_7_nights.description: Cross the bridge into Nassau for Bahamian conch fritters at Arawak Cay, browse Bay Street, see the Queen's Staircase. Back to The Cove for late afternoon at Cain, dinner at Nobu, then casino nightcap.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Slow morning and farewell
itinerary_7_nights.description: Breakfast in suite, last hour at Cove Beach, late checkout if available. Lunch at the Cove Pool Bar, then transfer to Nassau airport.

caveats: Atlantis is a sprawling resort destination — even at The Cove, you'll cross paths with families and conference groups in shared spaces (waterpark, casino, restaurants).
Cain pool is adults-only but the rest of the resort is family-friendly; if you want zero kids in your week, this is not the right pick.
Resort fees, parking, and waterpark wristbands push the all-in cost meaningfully above the headline rate — confirm what's bundled before booking.

faqs.question: Is The Cove actually adults-only?
faqs.answer: No — it's adults-preferred. The Cain at the Cove pool and beach loungers are 18+, but kids can stay in Cove suites and the broader Atlantis resort is family-oriented.

faqs.question: Is Aquaventure waterpark included?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Atlantis room categories including The Cove include unlimited Aquaventure access, pools, and beach throughout your stay.

faqs.question: How does The Cove differ from the Royal Towers?
faqs.answer: The Cove is a separate 600-room all-suite tower with its own lobby, pool, and beach. Royal Towers is the iconic family-focused property with the Bridge Suite and aquarium. The Cove feels calmer and more design-forward.

faqs.question: What's the best time of year for a Cove honeymoon?
faqs.answer: Mid-November through mid-December, then late April through early June, hits the sweet spot — warm water, low rain risk, fewer families than peak holiday and summer.

faqs.question: Can we get butler service at The Cove?
faqs.answer: Standard suites include concierge support; butler-level service kicks in at the Royal Reserve Suites and Sapphire-tier rooms with private check-in and dedicated attendants.

faqs.question: Is the casino part of the experience?
faqs.answer: The Atlantis casino is a quick walk from The Cove and is one of the largest in the Caribbean — table games, slots, a poker room, plus celebrity-chef restaurants surrounding it.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — The Cove at Atlantis Paradise Island

Hello,

We are planning our honeymoon at The Cove and would like to confirm availability and rates for a Cove Ocean Suite, high floor, ocean view, for 7 nights.

Could you please confirm:
- Honeymoon package inclusions (champagne, dinner credit, spa credit, room upgrade if available)
- Daily breakfast options and resort credit
- Cain at the Cove cabana reservation policy
- Dolphin Cay and Aquaventure inclusions
- Airport transfer arrangements from Nassau International

Thank you,
content.extra_notes: The Cove sits at the quieter eastern tip of Paradise Island. The walk to Aquaventure takes about 8 minutes through the resort, or you can use the internal shuttle. Couples often note that breakfast at the Cove Pool Bar is the best on-property casual meal.
